translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Proverbs of Solomon, son of David, king of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to know wisdom and instruction; to discern the words of understanding;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to receive the instruction of wisdom, righteousness and judgment, and equity;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to give prudence to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is wise will hear, and will increase learning; and the intelligent will gain wise counsels:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to understand a proverb and an allegory, the words of the wise and their enigmas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of knowledge: fools despise wisdom and instruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, my son, the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the teaching of thy mother;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for they shall be a garland of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, if sinners entice thee, consent not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If they say, Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, let us lurk secretly for the innocent without cause;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: let us swallow them up alive as Sheol, and whole, as those that go down into the pit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: we shall find all precious substance, we shall fill our houses with spoil:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: cast in thy lot among us; we will all have one purse:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — my son, walk not in the way with them, keep back thy foot from their path;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for their feet run to evil, and they make haste to shed blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in vain the net is spread in the sight of anything which hath wings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these lay wait for their own blood; they lurk secretly for their own lives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So are the paths of every one that is greedy of gain: it taketh away the life of its possessors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wisdom crieth without; she raiseth her voice in the broadways;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: she calleth in the chief [place] of concourse, in the entry of the gates; in the city she uttereth her words:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long, simple ones, will ye love simpleness, and scorners take pleasure in their scorning, and the foolish hate knowledge?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Turn you at my reproof: behold, I will pour forth my spirit unto you, I will make known to you my words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no one regarded;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye have rejected all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I also will laugh in your calamity, I will mock when your fear cometh;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when your fear cometh as sudden destruction, and your calamity cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish come upon you:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — then will they call upon me, but I will not answer; they will seek me early, and shall not find me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they would none of my counsel, they despised all my reproof:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their way, and be filled with their own devices.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of the foolish shall cause them to perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and shall be at rest from fear of evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, if thou receivest my words, and layest up my commandments with thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom [and] thou apply thy heart to understanding;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: yea, if thou criest after discernment [and] liftest up thy voice to understanding;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if thou seekest her as silver and searchest for her as for hidden treasures:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then shalt thou understand the fear of Jehovah, and find the knowledge ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah giveth wisdom; out of his mouth [come] knowledge and understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He layeth up sound wisdom for the upright; [he] is a shield to them that walk in integrity;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: guarding the paths of just judgment and keeping the way of hisgodly ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shalt thou understand righteousness and judgment and equity: every good path.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When wisdom entereth into thy heart and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: discretion shall keep thee, understanding shall preserve thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To deliver thee from the way of evil, from the man that speaketh froward things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [from those] who leave the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who rejoice to do evil, [and] delight in the frowardness of evil;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whose paths are crooked, and who are perverted in their course:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To deliver thee from the strange woman, from the stranger who flattereth with her words;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who forsaketh the guide of her youth, and forgetteth the covenant of herGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — for her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: none that go unto her return again, neither do they attain to the paths of life:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — that thou mayest walk in the way of the good, and keep the paths of the righteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the upright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remain in it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the wicked shall be cut off from the land, and the unfaithful shall be plucked up out of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, forget not my teaching, and let thy heart observe my commandments;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for length of days, and years of life, and peace shall they add to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not loving-kindness and truth forsake thee; bind them about thy neck, write them upon the tablet of thy heart:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt find favour and good understanding in the sight ofGod and man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Confide in Jehovah with all thy heart, and lean not unto thine own intelligence;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in all thy ways acknowledge him, and he will make plain thy paths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not wise in thine own eyes; fear Jehovah, and depart from evil:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: it shall be health for thy navel, and moisture for thy bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Honour Jehovah with thy substance, and with the first-fruits of all thine increase;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy vats shall overflow with new wine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, despise not the instruction of Jehovah, neither be weary of his chastisement;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for whom Jehovah loveth he chasteneth, even as a father the son in whom he delighteth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man that findeth wisdom, and the man that getteth understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the gain thereof is better than the gain of silver, and her revenue than fine gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She is more precious than rubies; and all the things thou canst desire are not equal unto her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Length of days is in her right hand; in her left hand riches and honour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her; and happy is he that retaineth her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding he established the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By his knowledge the deeps were broken up, and the skies drop down the dew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, let them not depart from thine eyes; keep sound wisdom and discretion:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so shall they be life unto thy soul, and grace unto thy neck.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shalt thou walk in thy way securely, and thy foot shall not stumble;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid, but thou shalt lie down and thy sleep shall be sweet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not afraid of sudden fear, neither of the destruction of the wicked, when it cometh;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Jehovah shall be thy confidence, and he will keep thy foot from being taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thy hand to do it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Say not unto thy neighbour, Go, and come again, and to-morrow I will give, when thou hast it by thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Devise not evil against thy neighbour, seeing he dwelleth securely by thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Strive not with a man without cause, if he have done thee no harm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Envy not the man of violence, and choose none of his ways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the perverse is an abomination to Jehovah; but his secret is with the upright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The curse of Jehovah is in the house of the wicked; but he blesseth the habitation of the righteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He indeed scorneth the scorners; but he giveth grace unto the lowly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wise shall inherit glory; but shame shall be the promotion of the foolish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend to know intelligence;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I give you good doctrine: forsake ye not my law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I was a son unto my father, tender and an only one in the sight of my mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he taught me, and said unto me, Let thy heart retain my words; keep my commandments and live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Get wisdom, get intelligence: forget [it] not; neither decline from the words of my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forsake her not, and she shall keep thee; love her, and she shall preserve thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The beginning of wisdom [is], Get wisdom; and with all thy getting get intelligence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Exalt her, and she shall promote thee; she shall bring thee to honour when thou dost embrace her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She shall give to thy head a garland of grace; a crown of glory will she bestow upon thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, my son, and receive my sayings, and the years of thy life shall be multiplied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will teach thee in the way of wisdom, I will lead thee in paths of uprightness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thou goest, thy steps shall not be straitened; and when thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take fast hold of instruction, let [her] not go: keep her, for she is thy life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Enter not into the path of the wicked, and go not in the way of evil [men]:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: avoid it, pass not by it; turn from it, and pass away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they sleep not except they have done mischief, and their sleep is taken away unless they have caused [some] to fall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the path of the righteous is as the shining light, going on and brightening until the day be fully come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thy heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep thy heart more than anything that is guarded; for out of it are the issues of life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Put away from thee perverseness of mouth, and corrupt lips put far from thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be well-ordered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Turn not to the right hand nor to the left; remove thy foot from evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, attend unto my wisdom, incline thine ear to my understanding;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that thou mayest keep reflection, and that thy lips may preserve knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the lips of the strange woman drop honey, and her mouth is smoother than oil;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but her end is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lest she should ponder the path of life, her ways wander, she knoweth not [whither].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, children, hearken unto me, and depart not from the words of my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest strangers be filled with thy wealth, and the fruits of thy toil [come] into the house of a stranger;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou mourn in thine end, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou say, How have I hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I have not hearkened unto the voice of my teachers, nor inclined mine ear to those that instructed me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was well nigh in all evil in the midst of the congregation and assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Drink waters out of thine own cistern, and running waters out of thine own well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy fountains shall be poured forth, as water-brooks in the broadways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them be only thine own, and not strangers' with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let thy fountain be blessed; and have joy of the wife of thy youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a lovely hind and a graceful roe, let her breasts satisfy thee at all times: be thou ravished continually with her love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And why shouldest thou, my son, be ravished with a strange woman, and embrace the bosom of a stranger?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the ways of man are before the eyes of Jehovah, and he pondereth all his paths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His own iniquities shall take the wicked, and he shall be holden with the cords of his sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall die for want of discipline; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, if thou hast become surety for thy friend, if thou hast stricken thy hand for a stranger,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou art snared with the words of thy mouth, thou art taken with the words of thy mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do this now, my son, and deliver thyself, since thou hast come into the hand of thy friend: go, humble thyself, and be urgent with thy friend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give not sleep to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: deliver thyself as a gazelle from the hand [of the hunter], and as a bird from the hand of the fowler.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways and be wise:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which having no chief, overseer, or ruler,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: provideth her bread in the summer, [and] gathereth her food in the harvest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long, sluggard, wilt thou lie down? When wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So shall thy poverty come as a roving plunderer, and thy penury as an armed man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man of Belial, a wicked person, is he that goeth about with a perverse mouth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feet, he teacheth with his fingers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: deceits are in his heart; he deviseth mischief at all times, he soweth discords.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly: in a moment shall he be broken, and without remedy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These six [things] doth Jehovah hate, yea, seven are an abomination unto him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a heart that deviseth wicked imaginations; feet that are swift in running to mischief;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a false witness that uttereth lies, and he that soweth discords among brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, observe thy father's commandment, and forsake not the teaching of thy mother;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: bind them continually upon thy heart, tie them about thy neck:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when thou walkest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and [when] thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the commandment is a lamp, and the teaching a light, and reproofs of instruction are the way of life:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to keep thee from the evil woman, from the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lust not after her beauty in thy heart, neither let her take thee with her eyelids;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for by means of a whorish woman [a man is brought] to a loaf of bread, and another's wife doth hunt for the precious soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Can a man take fire in his bosom, and his garments not be burned?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Can one go upon hot coals, and his feet not be scorched?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So he that goeth in to his neighbour's wife: whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They do not despise a thief, if he steal to satisfy his soul when he is hungry:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if he be found, he shall restore sevenfold; he shall give all the substance of his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso committeth adultery with a woman is void of understanding: he that doeth it destroyeth his own soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wound and contempt shall he get; and his reproach shall not be wiped away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For jealousy is the rage of a man, and he will not spare in the day of vengeance;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he will not regard any ransom, neither will he rest content though thou multipliest [thy] gifts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keep my commandments, and live; and my teaching, as the apple of thine eye.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the tablet of thy heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Say unto wisdom, Thou art my sister, and call intelligence [thy] kinswoman:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger who flattereth with her words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For at the window of my house, I looked forth through my lattice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the sons, a young man void of understanding,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in the twilight, in the evening of the day, in the blackness of night and the darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there met him a woman in the attire of a harlot, and subtle of heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She is clamorous and unmanageable; her feet abide not in her house:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: now without, now in the broadways, — and she lieth in wait at every corner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she caught him and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have peace-offerings with me; this day have I paid my vows:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore came I forth to meet thee, to seek earnestly thy face, and I have found thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have decked my bed with tapestry coverlets of variegated linen from Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have perfumed my couch with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come, let us revel in love until the morning, let us delight ourselves with loves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the husband is not at home, he is gone a long journey;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he hath taken the money-bag with him, he will come home on the day of the full moon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With her much enticement she beguiled him; with the smoothness of her lips she constrained him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He went after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter, and as stocks [serve] for the correction of the fool;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: till an arrow strike through his liver: as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it is for its life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, ye sons, hearken unto me, and attend to the words of my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not thy heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for she hath cast down many wounded, and all slain by her were strong.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her house is the way to Sheol, going down to the chambers of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth not wisdom cry? and understanding give forth her voice?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the top of high places by the way, at the cross-paths she taketh her stand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Beside the gates, at the entry of the city, at the coming in at the doors, she crieth aloud.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unto you, men, I call, and my voice is to the sons of man:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O ye simple, understand prudence; and ye foolish, understand sense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, for I will speak excellent things, and the opening of my lips shall be right things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my palate shall meditate truth, and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing tortuous or perverse in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are all plain to him that understandeth, and right to them that find knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for wisdom is better than rubies, and all the things that may be desired are not equal to it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I wisdom dwell [with] prudence, and find the knowledge [which cometh] of reflection.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah is to hate evil; pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth do I hate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom: I am intelligence; I have strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By me kings reign, and rulers make just decrees;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: by me princes rule, and nobles, all the judges of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I love those that love me; and they that seek me early shall find me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Riches and honour are with me; durable wealth and righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My fruit is better than fine gold, yea, than pure gold; and my revenue than choice silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I walk in the path of righteousness, in the midst of the paths of judgment:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that I may cause those that love me to inherit substance; and I will fill their treasuries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was set up from eternity, from the beginning, before the earth was.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When there were no depths, I was brought forth, when there were no fountains abounding with water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before the mountains were settled, before the hills was I brought forth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: while as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the beginning of the dust of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When he prepared the heavens I was there; when he ordained the circle upon the face of the deep;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when he established the skies above, when the fountains of the deep became strong;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when he imposed on the sea his decree that the waters should not pass his commandment, when he appointed the foundations of the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then I was by him [his] nursling, and I was daily his delight, rejoicing always before him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth, and my delights [were] with the sons of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, sons, hearken unto me, and blessed are they that keep my ways:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: hear instruction and be wise, and refuse it not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man that heareth me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whoso findeth me findeth life, and obtaineth favour of Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he that sinneth against me doeth violence to his own soul: all they that hate me love death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wisdom hath built her house, she hath hewn out her seven pillars;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: she hath slaughtered her cattle, she hath mingled her wine, she hath also prepared her table;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: she hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the summits of the high places of the city,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither. To him that is void of understanding, she saith,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come, eat ye of my bread, and drink of the wine that I have mingled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forsake follies and live, and go in the way of intelligence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that instructeth a scorner getteth to himself shame; and he that reproveth a wicked [man] [getteth] to himself a blot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee; reprove a wise [man], and he will love thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Impart to a wise [man], and he will become yet wiser; teach a righteous [man], and he will increase learning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of wisdom; and the knowledge of the Holy is intelligence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and years of life shall be added to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou art wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself; and if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The foolish woman is clamorous; she is stupid, and knoweth nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she sitteth at the entry of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to call passers-by who go right on their ways:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither. And to him that is void of understanding she saith,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Stolen waters are sweet, and the bread of secrecy is pleasant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he knoweth not that the dead are there; [that] her guests are in the depths of Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father; but a foolish son is the grief of his mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing; but righteousness delivereth from death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah suffereth not the soul of the righteous [man] to famish; but he repelleth the craving of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He cometh to want that dealeth with a slack hand; but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that gathereth in summer is a wise son; he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessings are upon the head of a righteous [man]; but the mouth of the wicked covereth violence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The memory of the righteous [man] shall be blessed; but the name of the wicked shall rot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wise in heart receiveth commandments; but a prating fool shall fall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that walketh in integrity walketh securely; but he that perverteth his ways shall be known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that winketh with the eye causeth grief, and a prating fool shall fall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mouth of a righteous [man] is a fountain of life; but the mouth of the wicked covereth violence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hatred stirreth up strifes; but love covereth all transgressions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the lips of an intelligent [man] wisdom is found; but a rod is for the back of him that is void of understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wise lay up knowledge; but the mouth of the fool is near destruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The rich man's wealth is his strong city; the destruction of the poor is their poverty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The labour of a righteous [man] [tendeth] to life; the revenue of a wicked [man], to sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Keeping instruction is the path to life; but he that forsaketh reproof goeth astray.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that covereth hatred hath lying lips, and he that sendeth forth a slander is a fool.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the multitude of words there wanteth not transgression; but he that restraineth his lips doeth wisely.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The tongue of the righteous [man] is [as] choice silver; the heart of the wicked is little worth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lips of a righteous [man] feed many; but fools die for want of understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The blessing of Jehovah, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow to it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is as sport to a foolish [man] to do wickedness; but a man of understanding hath wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of a wicked [man], it shall come upon him; but the desire of the righteous shall be granted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a whirlwind passeth, so is the wicked no [more]; but the righteous is an everlasting foundation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to them that send him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah prolongeth days; but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The hope of the righteous is joy; but the expectation of the wicked shall perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The way of Jehovah is strength to the perfect [man], but destruction to the workers of iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous [man] shall never be moved; but the wicked shall not inhabit the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mouth of a righteous [man] putteth forth wisdom; but the froward tongue shall be cut out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lips of a righteous [man] know what is acceptable; but the mouth of the wicked is frowardness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A false balance is an abomination to Jehovah; but a just weight is his delight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [When] pride cometh, then cometh shame; but with the lowly is wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The integrity of the upright guideth them; but the crookedness of the unfaithful destroyeth them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wealth profiteth not in the day of wrath; but righteousness delivereth from death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteousness of the perfect maketh plain his way; but the wicked falleth by his own wickedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteousness of the upright delivereth them; but the treacherous are taken in their own craving.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When a wicked man dieth, [his] expectation shall perish; and the hope of evil [men] perisheth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked cometh in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With his mouth a hypocrite destroyeth his neighbour; but through knowledge are the righteous delivered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When it goeth well with the righteous, the city rejoiceth; and when the wicked perish, there is shouting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted; but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that despiseth his neighbour is void of heart; but a man of understanding holdeth his peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that goeth about talebearing revealeth secrets; but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where no advice is, the people fall; but in the multitude of counsellors there is safety.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It goeth ill with him that is surety for another; but he that hateth suretyship is secure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A gracious woman retaineth honour; and the violent retain riches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The merciful man doeth good to his own soul; but the cruel troubleth his own flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked worketh a deceitful work; but he that soweth righteousness hath a sure reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As righteousness [tendeth] to life, so he that pursueth evil [doeth it] to his own death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The perverse in heart are abomination to Jehovah; but they that are perfect in [their] way are his delight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hand for hand! an evil [man] shall not be held innocent; but the seed of the righteous shall be delivered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A fair woman who is without discretion, is [as] a gold ring in a swine's snout.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The desire of the righteous is only good; the expectation of the wicked is wrath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth; and there is that withholdeth more than is right, but [it tendeth] only to want.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The liberal soul shall be made fat, and he that watereth shall be watered also himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that withholdeth corn, the people curse him; but blessing shall be upon the head of him that selleth it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is earnest after good seeketh favour; but he that searcheth for mischief, it shall come upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that trusteth in his riches shall fall; but the righteous shall flourish as a leaf.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that troubleth his own house shall inherit wind; and the fool shall be servant to the wise of heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and the wise winneth souls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the righteous shall be requited on the earth: how much more the wicked and the sinner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso loveth discipline loveth knowledge, but he that hateth reproof is brutish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A good [man] obtaineth favour of Jehovah; but a man of mischievous devices will he condemn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man shall not be established by wickedness; but the root of the righteous shall not be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A woman of worth is a crown to her husband; but she that maketh ashamed is as rottenness in his bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The thoughts of the righteous are right; the counsels of the wicked are deceit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of the wicked are a lying-in-wait for blood; but the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Overthrow the wicked, and they are no [more]; but the house of the righteous shall stand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man is commended according to his wisdom; but he that is of a perverted heart shall be despised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Better is he that is lightly esteemed, and hath a servant, than he that honoureth himself, and lacketh bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A righteous man is concerned for the life of his beast; but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread; but he that followeth the worthless is void of understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked desireth the net of evil [men]; but the root of the righteous yieldeth [fruit].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the transgression of the lips is an evil snare; but a righteous [man] shall go forth out of trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man is satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth; and the recompense of a man's hands shall be rendered unto him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes; but he that is wise hearkeneth unto counsel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The vexation of the fool is presently known; but a prudent [man] covereth shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that uttereth truth sheweth forth righteousness; but a false witness deceit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is that babbleth like the piercings of a sword; but the tongue of the wise is health.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lip of truth shall be established for ever; but a lying tongue is but for a moment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deceit is in the heart of them that devise evil; but to the counsellors of peace is joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There shall no evil happen to a righteous [man]; but the wicked shall be filled with mischief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lying lips are an abomination to Jehovah; but they that deal truly are his delight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A prudent man concealeth knowledge; but the heart of the foolish proclaimeth folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The hand of the diligent shall bear rule; but the slothful [hand] shall be under tribute.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop; but a good word maketh it glad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous guideth his neighbour; but the way of the wicked misleadeth them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The slothful roasteth not what he took in hunting; but man's precious substance is to the diligent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the path of righteousness is life, and in the pathway thereof there is no death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wise son [heareth] his father's instruction; but a scorner heareth not rebuke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man shall eat good by the fruit of his mouth; but the soul of the treacherous, violence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that guardeth his mouth keepeth his soul; destruction shall be to him that openeth wide his lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A sluggard's soul desireth and hath nothing; but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A righteous [man] hateth lying; but the wicked maketh himself odious and cometh to shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Righteousness preserveth him that is perfect in the way; but wickedness overthroweth the sinner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is that feigneth himself rich, and hath nothing; there is that maketh himself poor, and hath great wealth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The ransom of a man's life is his riches; but the indigent heareth not rebuke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The light of the righteous rejoiceth; but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By pride there only cometh contention; but with the well-advised is wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wealth [gotten] by vanity diminisheth; but he that gathereth by manual-labour shall increase [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hope deferred maketh the heart sick; but the desire [that] cometh to pass is a tree of life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso despiseth the word shall be held by it; but he that feareth the commandment shall be rewarded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The teaching of the wise [man] is a fountain of life, to turn away from the snares of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Good understanding procureth favour; but the way of the treacherous is hard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every prudent [man] acteth with knowledge; but the foolish layeth open [his] folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wicked messenger falleth into evil; but a faithful ambassador is health.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Poverty and shame shall be [to] him that refuseth instruction; but he that regardeth reproof shall be honoured.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The desire accomplished is sweet to the soul; but it is abomination to the foolish to depart from evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that walketh with wise [men] becometh wise; but a companion of the foolish will be depraved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Evil pursueth sinners; but to the righteous good shall be repaid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A good man leaveth an inheritance to his children's children; but the wealth of the sinner is laid up for the righteous [man].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Much food is in the tillage of the poor; but there is that is lost for want of judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that spareth his rod hateth his son; but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul; but the belly of the wicked shall want.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wisdom of women buildeth their house; but folly plucketh it down with her hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that walketh in his uprightness feareth Jehovah; but he that is perverted in his ways despiseth him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the fool's mouth is a rod of pride; but the lips of the wise shall preserve them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where no oxen are, the crib is clean; but much increase is by the strength of the ox.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A faithful witness will not lie; but a false witness uttereth lies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A scorner seeketh wisdom, and there is none [for him]; but knowledge is easy unto the intelligent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go from the presence of a foolish man, in whom thou perceivest not the lips of knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wisdom of the prudent is to discern his way; but the folly of the foolish is deceit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fools make a mock at trespass; but for the upright there is favour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heart knoweth its own bitterness, and a stranger doth not intermeddle with its joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The house of the wicked shall be overthrown; but the tent of the upright shall flourish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof is the ways of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful, and the end of mirth is sadness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The backslider in heart shall be filled with his own ways, and the good man from what is in himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The simple believeth every word; but the prudent [man] heedeth his going.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wise [man] feareth and departeth from evil; but the foolish is overbearing and confident.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is soon angry dealeth foolishly, and a man of mischievous devices is hated.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The simple inherit folly; but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The evil bow before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous [man].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is poor is hated even of his own neighbour; but the rich hath many friends.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth; but he that is gracious to the afflicted, happy is he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do they not err that devise evil? but loving-kindness and truth are for those that devise good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In all labour there is profit; but the talk of the lips [tendeth] only to want.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The crown of the wise is their riches; the folly of the foolish is folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A true witness delivereth souls; but deceit uttereth lies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the fear of Jehovah is strong confidence, and his children shall have a place of refuge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah is a fountain of life, to turn away from the snares of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the multitude of people is the king's glory; but in the lack of people is the ruin of a prince.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is slow to anger is of great understanding; but he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A sound heart is the life of the flesh; but envy the rottenness of the bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker; but he that honoureth Him is gracious to the needy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked is driven away by his evil-doing; but the righteous trusteth, [even] in his death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wisdom resteth in the heart of the intelligent [man]; but that which is in the foolish maketh itself known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Righteousness exalteth a nation; but sin is a reproach to peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The king's favour is toward a wise servant; but his wrath is [against] him that causeth shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A soft answer turneth away fury; but a grievous word stirreth up anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The tongue of the wise useth knowledge aright; but the mouth of the foolish poureth out folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eyes of Jehovah are in every place, beholding the evil and the good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gentleness of tongue is a tree of life; but crookedness therein is a breaking of the spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A fool despiseth his father's instruction; but he that regardeth reproof becometh prudent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the house of a righteous [man] is much treasure; but in the revenue of a wicked [man] is disturbance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lips of the wise disperse knowledge, but not so the heart of the foolish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to Jehovah; but the prayer of the upright is his delight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The way of a wicked [man] is an abomination to Jehovah; but him that pursueth righteousness he loveth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Grievous correction is for him that forsaketh the path; he that hateth reproof shall die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sheol and destruction are before Jehovah; how much more then the hearts of the children of men!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A scorner loveth not one that reproveth him; he will not go unto the wise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A joyful heart maketh a cheerful countenance; but by sorrow of heart the spirit is broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heart of an intelligent [man] seeketh knowledge; but the mouth of the foolish feedeth on folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the days of the afflicted are evil; but a cheerful heart is a continual feast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Better is little with the fear of Jehovah than great store and disquietude therewith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Better is a meal of herbs where love is, than a fatted ox and hatred therewith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A furious man stirreth up contention; but he that is slow to anger appeaseth strife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The way of the sluggard is as a hedge of thorns; but the path of the upright is made plain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wise son maketh a glad father; but a foolish man despiseth his mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Folly is joy to him that is void of sense; but a man of understanding regulateth his walk.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Without counsel purposes are disappointed; but in the multitude of counsellors they are established.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man hath joy by the answer of his mouth; and a word in its season, how good is it!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The path of life is upwards for the wise, that he may depart from Sheol beneath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah plucketh up the house of the proud; but he establisheth the boundary of the widow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The thoughts of the evil [man] are an abomination to Jehovah; but pure words are pleasant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house; but he that hateth gifts shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heart of a righteous [man] studieth to answer; but the mouth of the wicked poureth out evil things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is far from the wicked; but he heareth the prayer of the righteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That which enlighteneth the eyes rejoiceth the heart; good tidings make the bones fat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The ear that heareth the reproof of life shall abide among the wise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that refuseth instruction despiseth his own soul; but he that heareth reproof getteth sense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah is the discipline of wisdom, and before honour [goeth] humility.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The purposes of the heart are of man, but the answer of the tongue is from Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but Jehovah weigheth the spirits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Commit thy works unto Jehovah, and thy thoughts shall be established.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath wrought everything on his own account, yea, even the wicked for the day of evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every proud heart is an abomination to Jehovah: hand for hand, he shall not be held innocent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By loving-kindness and truth iniquity is atoned for; and by the fear of Jehovah [men] depart from evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When a man's ways please Jehovah, he maketh even his enemies to be at peace with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Better is a little with righteousness, than great revenues without right.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heart of man deviseth his way, but Jehovah directeth his steps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An oracle is on the lips of the king: his mouth will not err in judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The just balance and scales are Jehovah's; all the weights of the bag are his work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is an abomination to kings to commit wickedness; for the throne is established by righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Righteous lips are the delight of kings, and they love him that speaketh aright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fury of a king is [as] messengers of death; but a wise man will pacify it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the light of the king's countenance is life, and his favour is as a cloud of the latter rain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How much better is it to get wisdom than fine gold, and the getting of intelligence to be preferred to silver!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil: he that taketh heed to his way keepeth his soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pride [goeth] before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Better is it to be of a humble spirit with the meek, than to divide the spoil with the proud.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that giveth heed to the word shall find good; and whoso confideth in Jehovah, happy is he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wise in heart is called intelligent, and the sweetness of the lips increaseth learning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wisdom is a fountain of life for him that hath it; but the instruction of fools is folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heart of the wise maketh his mouth intelligent, and upon his lips increaseth learning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pleasant words are [as] a honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health for the bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof is the ways of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The appetite of the labourer laboureth for him, for his mouth urgeth him on.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man of Belial diggeth up evil, and on his lips there is as a scorching fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A false man soweth contention; and a talebearer separateth very friends.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A violent man enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him into a way that is not good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that shutteth his eyes, [it is] to devise froward things; biting his lips, he bringeth evil to pass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The hoary head is a crown of glory, [if] it is found in the way of righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lot is cast into the lap; but the whole decision is of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than a house full of feasting [with] strife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wise servant shall rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part in the inheritance among the brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fining-pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold; but Jehovah trieth the hearts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The evil-doer giveth heed to iniquitous lips; the liar giveth ear to a mischievous tongue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso mocketh a poor [man] reproacheth his Maker; he that is glad at calamity shall not be held innocent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Children's children are the crown of old men; and the glory of children are their fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Excellent speech becometh not a vile [man]; how much less do lying lips a noble!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A gift is a precious stone in the eyes of the possessor: whithersoever it turneth it prospereth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that covereth transgression seeketh love; but he that bringeth a matter up again separateth very friends.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A reproof entereth more deeply into him that hath understanding than a hundred stripes into a fool.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An evil [man] seeketh only rebellion; but a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let a bear robbed of her whelps meet a man rather than a fool in his folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The beginning of contention is [as] when one letteth out water; therefore leave off strife before it become vehement.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that justifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the righteous, even they both are abomination to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To what purpose is there a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing [he] hath no sense?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A senseless man striketh hands, becoming surety for his neighbour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He loveth transgression that loveth a quarrel; he that maketh high his gate seeketh destruction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hath a perverse heart findeth no good; and he that shifteth about with his tongue falleth into evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that begetteth a fool [doeth it] to his sorrow, and the father of a vile [man] hath no joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A joyful heart promoteth healing; but a broken spirit drieth up the bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wicked [man] taketh a gift out of the bosom, to pervert the paths of judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A foolish son is a grief to his father, and bitterness to her that bore him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To punish a righteous [man] is not good, nor to strike nobles because of [their] uprightness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hath knowledge spareth his words; and a man of understanding is of a cool spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even a fool when he holdeth his peace is reckoned wise, [and] he that shutteth his lips, intelligent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that separateth himself seeketh [his] pleasure, he is vehement against all sound wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A fool hath no delight in understanding, but only that his heart may reveal itself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the wicked cometh, there cometh also contempt, and with ignominy reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of a man's mouth are deep waters, [and] the fountain of wisdom is a gushing brook.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is not good to accept the person of the wicked, to wrong the righteous in judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A fool's lips enter into contention, and his mouth calleth for stripes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A fool's mouth is destruction to him, and his lips are a snare to his soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of a talebearer are as dainty morsels, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He also who is indolent in his work is brother of the destroyer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The name of Jehovah is a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The rich man's wealth is his strong city, and as a high wall in his own imagination.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before destruction the heart of man is haughty; and before honour [goeth] humility.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that giveth answer before he heareth, it is folly and shame unto him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The spirit of a man sustaineth his infirmity; but a broken spirit who can bear?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heart of an intelligent [man] getteth knowledge, and the ear of the wise seeketh knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man's gift maketh room for him, and bringeth him before great men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is first in his own cause [seemeth] just; but his neighbour cometh and searcheth him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lot causeth contentions to cease, and parteth between the mighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A brother offended is [harder to be won] than a strong city; and contentions are as the bars of a palace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man's belly is satisfied with the fruit of his mouth; with the increase of his lips is he satisfied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso hath found a wife hath found a good thing, and hath obtained favour from Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is poor speaketh with supplications, but the rich answereth roughly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man of [many] friends will come to ruin but there is a friend [that] sticketh closer than a brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Better is a poor [man] that walketh in his integrity, than he that is perverse in his lips, and is a fool.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also that a person be without knowledge is not good; and he that hasteth with his feet maketh false steps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The folly of man distorteth his way, and his heart is irritated against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wealth addeth many friends; but the poor is separated from his friend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A false witness shall not be held innocent, and he that uttereth lies shall not escape.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many court the favour of a noble; and every one is friend to a man that giveth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the brethren of a poor [man] hate him; how much more do his friends go far from him: he pursueth [them] with words, — they are not [to be found].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that getteth sense loveth his own soul; he that keepeth understanding shall find good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A false witness shall not be held innocent, and he that uttereth lies shall perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Good living beseemeth not a fool; how much less for a servant to have rule over princes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The discretion of a man maketh him slow to anger, and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The king's displeasure is as the roaring of a lion; but his favour is as dew upon the grass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A foolish son is the calamity of his father; and the contentions of a wife are a continual dropping.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: House and wealth are an inheritance from fathers; but a prudent wife is from Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Slothfulness casteth into a deep sleep, and the idle soul shall suffer hunger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his soul; he that is careless of his ways shall die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is gracious to the poor lendeth unto Jehovah; and what he hath bestowed will he repay unto him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Chasten thy son, seeing there is hope; but set not thy soul upon killing him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man of great wrath shall suffer punishment; for if thou deliver [him], yet thou must do it again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many are the thoughts in a man's heart, but the counsel of Jehovah, that doth stand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The charm of a man is his kindness; and a poor [man] is better than a liar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah [tendeth] to life, and he [that hath it] shall rest satisfied without being visited with evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A sluggard burieth his hand in the dish, and will not even bring it to his mouth again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Smite a scorner, and the simple will beware; reprove the intelligent, and he will understand knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that ruineth [his] father and chaseth away [his] mother is a son that causeth shame and bringeth reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cease, my son, to hear the instruction which causeth to stray from the words of knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A witness of Belial scorneth judgment, and the mouth of the wicked swalloweth down iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judgments are prepared for scorners, and stripes for the back of the foolish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wine is a scorner, strong drink is raging; and whoso erreth thereby is not wise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The terror of a king is as the roaring of a lion: he that provoketh him to anger sinneth against his own soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is an honour for a man to cease from strife; but every fool rusheth into it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sluggard will not plough by reason of the winter; he shall beg in harvest, and have nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Counsel in the heart of man is deep water, and a man of understanding draweth it out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Most men will proclaim every one his own kindness; but a faithful man who shall find?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous walketh in his integrity: blessed are his children after him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A king sitting on the throne of judgment scattereth away all evil with his eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Divers weights, divers measures, even both of them are abomination to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even a child is known by his doings, whether his work be pure, and whether it be right.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, Jehovah hath made even both of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty; open thine eyes, [and] thou shalt be satisfied with bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bad! bad! saith the buyer; but when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is gold, and a multitude of rubies; but the lips of knowledge are a precious Jewel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take his garment that is become surety [for] another, and hold him in pledge for strangers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bread of falsehood is sweet to a man, but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Plans are established by counsel; and with good advice make war.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that goeth about talebearing revealeth secrets; therefore meddle not with him that openeth his lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso curseth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in the blackest darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An inheritance obtained hastily at the beginning will not be blessed in the end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Say not, I will recompense evil: wait on Jehovah, and he shall save thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Divers weights are an abomination unto Jehovah; and a false balance is not good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The steps of a man are from Jehovah; and how can a man understand his own way?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is a snare to a man rashly to say, It is hallowed, and after vows to make inquiry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth the wheel over them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Man's spirit is the lamp of Jehovah, searching all the inner parts of the belly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mercy and truth preserve the king; and he upholdeth his throne by mercy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The glory of young men is their strength; and the beauty of old men is the grey head.
