translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wounding stripes purge away evil, and strokes [purge] the inner parts of the belly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The king's heart in the hand of Jehovah is [as] brooks of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes; but Jehovah weigheth the hearts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To exercise justice and judgment is more acceptable to Jehovah than sacrifice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lofty eyes, and a proud heart, the lamp of the wicked, is sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The thoughts of the diligent [tend] only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty, only to want.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The getting of treasures by a lying tongue is a fleeting breath of them that seek death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The devastation of the wicked sweepeth them away, because they refuse to do what is right.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Very crooked is the way of a guilty man; but as for the pure, his work is upright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a contentious woman, and a house in common.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The soul of the wicked desireth evil: his neighbour findeth no favour in his eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the scorner is punished, the simple becometh wise; and when the wise is instructed, he receiveth knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One that is righteous wisely considereth the house of the wicked: he overthroweth the wicked to [their] ruin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also himself shall cry, and shall not be heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A gift in secret pacifieth anger; and a present in the bosom, vehement fury.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is joy to a righteous [man] to do what is right; but it is ruin for the workers of iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The man that wandereth out of the way of wisdom shall abide in the congregation of the dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that loveth mirth shall be a poor man; he that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous, and a treacherous [man] in the stead of the upright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is better to dwell in a desert land. than with a contentious and irritable woman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is costly store and oil in the dwelling of a wise [man]; but a foolish man swalloweth it up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wise [man] scaleth the city of the mighty, and casteth down the strength of the confidence thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Proud, arrogant, scorner is his name who dealeth in proud wrath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The desire of the sluggard killeth him; for his hands refuse to work:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He coveteth greedily all the day long; but the righteous giveth and spareth not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sacrifice of the wicked is abomination: how much more when they bring it with a wicked purpose!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A lying witness shall perish; and a man that heareth shall speak constantly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wicked man hardeneth his face; but as for the upright, he establisheth his way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is no wisdom, nor understanding, nor counsel against Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The horse is prepared for the day of battle; but safety is of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A [good] name is rather to be chosen than great riches; loving favour rather than silver and gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The rich and poor meet together; Jehovah is the maker of them all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A prudent [man] seeth the evil, and hideth himself; but the simple pass on, and are punished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The reward of humility [and] the fear of Jehovah is riches, and honour, and life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thorns [and] snares are in the way of the perverse: he that keepeth his soul holdeth himself far from them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Train up the child according to the tenor of his way, and when he is old he will not depart from it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The rich ruleth over the poor; and the borrower is servant to the lender.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that soweth unrighteousness shall reap iniquity, and the rod of his wrath shall have an end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hath a bountiful eye shall be blessed, for he giveth of his bread to the poor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cast out the scorner, and contention will depart, and strife and ignominy shall cease.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that loveth pureness of heart, upon whose lips is grace, the king is his friend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eyes of Jehovah preserve knowledge; but he overthroweth the words of the unfaithful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sluggard saith, There is a lion without, I shall be killed in the streets!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mouth of strange women is a deep ditch: he with whom Jehovah is displeased shall fall therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Folly is bound in the heart of a child; the rod of correction shall drive it far from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that oppresseth the poor, it is to enrich him; he that giveth to the rich, [bringeth] only to want.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Incline thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thy heart unto my knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee: they shall be together fitted on thy lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That thy confidence may be in Jehovah, I have made [them] known to thee this day, even to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have not I written to thee excellent things, in counsels and knowledge,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest carry back words of truth to them that send thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rob not the poor, because he is poor, neither oppress the afflicted in the gate;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Jehovah will plead their cause, and despoil the soul of those that despoil them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make no friendship with an angry man, and go not with a furious man;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest thou learn his paths, and get a snare to thy soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not of them that strike hands, of them that are sureties for debts:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if thou hast nothing to pay, why should he take away thy bed from under thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remove not the ancient landmark which thy fathers have set.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou seen a man diligent in his work? He shall stand before kings; he shall not stand before the mean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thou sittest to eat with a ruler, consider well who is before thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and put a knife to thy throat, if thou be a man given to appetite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not desirous of his dainties; for they are deceitful food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Weary not thyself to become rich; cease from thine own intelligence:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: wilt thou set thine eyes upon it, it is gone; for indeed it maketh itself wings and it flieth away as an eagle towards the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Eat thou not the food of him that hath an evil eye, neither desire thou his dainties.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as he thinketh in his soul, so is he. Eat and drink! will he say unto thee; but his heart is not with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy morsel which thou hast eaten must thou vomit up, and thou wilt have wasted thy sweet words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak not in the ears of a foolish [man], for he will despise the wisdom of thy words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remove not the ancient landmark; and enter not into the fields of the fatherless:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for their redeemer is mighty; he will plead their cause against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Apply thy heart unto instruction, and thine ears to the words of knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Withhold not correction from the child; for [if] thou beatest him with the rod, he shall not die:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou shalt beat him with the rod, and shalt deliver his soul from Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, if thy heart be wise, my heart shall rejoice, even mine;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and my reins shall exult, when thy lips speak right things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not thy heart envy sinners, but [be thou] in the fear of Jehovah all the day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for surely there is a result, and thine expectation shall not be cut off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, my son, hear and be wise, and direct thy heart in the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not among winebibbers, among riotous eaters of flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the drunkard and the glutton shall come to poverty; and drowsiness clotheth with rags.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hearken unto thy father that begat thee, and despise not thy mother when she is old.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Buy the truth, and sell it not; wisdom, and instruction, and intelligence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The father of a righteous [man] shall greatly rejoice, and he that begetteth a wise [son] shall have joy of him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: let thy father and thy mother have joy, and let her that bore thee rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, give me thy heart, and let thine eyes observe my ways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a whore is a deep ditch; and a strange woman is a narrow pit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She also lieth in wait as a robber, and increaseth the treacherous among men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath woe? Who hath sorrow? Who contentions? Who complaining? Who wounds without cause? Who redness of eyes?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — They that tarry long at the wine; they that go to try mixed wine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Look not upon the wine when it is red, when it sparkleth in the cup, and goeth down smoothly:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: at the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine eyes shall behold strange women, and thy heart shall speak froward things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt be as he that lieth down in the midst of the sea, and as he that lieth down upon the top of a mast:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — “They have smitten me, [and] I am not sore; they have beaten me, [and] I knew it not. When shall I awake? I will seek it yet again.”

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not thou envious of evil men, neither desire to be with them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for their heart studieth destruction, and their lips talk of mischief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Through wisdom is a house built, and by understanding it is established;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and by knowledge are the chambers filled with all precious and pleasant substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wise man is strong, and a man of knowledge increaseth strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For with good advice shalt thou make thy war; and in the multitude of counsellors there is safety.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wisdom is too high for a fool: he will not open his mouth in the gate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that deviseth to do evil shall be called a master of intrigues.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The purpose of folly is sin, and the scorner is an abomination to men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [If] thou losest courage in the day of trouble, thy strength is small.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver them that are taken forth unto death, and withdraw not from them that stagger to slaughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not, will not he that weigheth the hearts consider it? And he that preserveth thy soul, he knoweth it; and he rendereth to man according to his work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Eat honey, my son, for it is good; and a honeycomb is sweet to thy taste:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so consider wisdom for thy soul; if thou hast found it, there shall be a result, and thine expectation shall not be cut off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lay not wait, O wicked [man], against the dwelling of the righteous; lay not waste his resting-place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the righteous falleth seven times, and riseth up again; but the wicked stumble into disaster.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth, and let not thy heart be glad when he stumbleth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest Jehovah see it, and it be evil in his sight, and he turn away his anger from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fret not thyself because of evil-doers, [and] be not envious of the wicked:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for there shall be no future to the evil [man]; the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My son, fear Jehovah and the king: meddle not with them that are given to change.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For their calamity shall rise suddenly; and who knoweth the ruin of them both?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things also come from the wise. It is not good to have respect of persons in judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that saith unto the wicked, Thou art righteous, peoples shall curse him, nations shall abhor him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but to them that rebuke [him] shall be delight, and a good blessing cometh upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He kisseth the lips who giveth a right answer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Prepare thy work without, and put thy field in order, and afterwards build thy house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not a witness against thy neighbour without cause; and wouldest thou deceive with thy lips?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Say not, I will do so to him as he hath done to me, I will render to the man according to his work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I went by the field of a sluggard, and by the vineyard of a man void of understanding;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and lo, it was all grown over with thistles, and nettles had covered the face thereof, and its stone wall was broken down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then I looked, I took it to heart; I saw, I received instruction:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So shall thy poverty come [as] a roving plunderer, and thy penury as an armed man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are also proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah transcribed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is the glory ofGod to conceal a thing; but the glory of kings is to search out a thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heavens for height, and the earth for depth, and the heart of kings is unsearchable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take away the dross from the silver, and there cometh forth a vessel for the refiner:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: take away the wicked from before the king, and his throne shall be established in righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Put not thyself forward in the presence of the king, and stand not in the place of the great;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for better it is that it be said unto thee, Come up hither, than that thou shouldest be put lower in the presence of the prince whom thine eyes see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go not forth hastily to strive, lest [thou know not] what to do in the end thereof, when thy neighbour hath put thee to shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Debate thy cause with thy neighbour, but reveal not the secret of another;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest he that heareth [it] disgrace thee, and thine evil report turn not away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] apples of gold in pictures of silver, is a word spoken in season.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An ear-ring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, is a wise reprover upon an attentive ear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest, [so] is a faithful messenger to them that send him: for he refresheth the soul of his masters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Clouds and wind without rain, [so] is a man that boasteth himself of a false gift.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By long forbearing is a ruler persuaded, and a soft tongue breaketh the bone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou found honey? Eat so much as is sufficient for thee, lest thou be surfeited therewith, and vomit it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let thy foot be seldom in thy neighbour's house; lest he be weary of thee and hate thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A maul, and a sword, and a sharp arrow, is a man that beareth false witness against his neighbour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A broken tooth, and a tottering foot, is confidence in an unfaithful [man] in the day of trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] he that taketh off a garment in cold weather, [and as] vinegar upon nitre, so is he that singeth songs to a sad heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he be thirsty, give him water to drink:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for thou shalt heap coals of fire upon his head, and Jehovah shall reward thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The north wind bringeth forth rain, and the angry countenance a backbiting tongue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a contentious woman, and a house in common.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] cold waters to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A troubled fountain, and a defiled well, is a righteous [man] that giveth way before the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is not good to eat much honey; and to search into weighty matters is [itself] a weight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hath no rule over his own spirit is [as] a city broken down, without walls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honour beseemeth not a fool.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As the sparrow for flitting about, as the swallow for flying, so a curse undeserved shall not come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the back of fools.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that sendeth a message by the hand of a fool cutteth off [his own] feet, [and] drinketh damage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The legs of the lame hang loose; so is a proverb in the mouth of fools.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a bag of gems in a stoneheap, so is he that giveth honour to a fool.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] a thorn goeth up into the hand of a drunkard, so is a proverb in the mouth of fools.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A master roughly worketh every one: he both hireth the fool and hireth passers-by.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a dog turneth back to its vomit, [so] a fool repeateth his folly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou seen a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope of a fool than of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sluggard saith, There is a fierce lion in the way; a lion is in the midst of the streets!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] the door turneth upon its hinges, so the sluggard upon his bed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sluggard burieth his hand in the dish: it wearieth him to bring it again to his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A sluggard is wiser in his own eyes than seven [men] that answer discreetly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that passing by vexeth himself with strife belonging not to him, is [like] one that taketh a dog by the ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a madman who casteth firebrands, arrows, and death,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so is a man that deceiveth his neighbour, and saith, Am I not in sport?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where no wood is, the fire goeth out; and where there is no talebearer, the contention ceaseth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As] coals for hot coals, and wood for fire, so is a contentious man to inflame strife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of a talebearer are as dainty morsels, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ardent lips, and a wicked heart, are [as] an earthen vessel overlaid with silver dross.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hateth dissembleth with his lips, but he layeth up deceit within him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when his voice is gracious, believe him not, for there are seven abominations in his heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though [his] hatred is covered by dissimulation, his wickedness shall be made manifest in the congregation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein; and he that rolleth a stone, it shall return upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A lying tongue hateth those that are injured by it, and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Boast not thyself of to-morrow, for thou knowest not what a day will bring forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let another praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A stone is heavy, and the sand weighty; but a fool's vexation is heavier than them both.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fury is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before jealousy?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Open rebuke is better than hidden love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are profuse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The full soul trampleth on a honeycomb; but to the hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so is a man that wandereth from his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ointment and perfume rejoice the heart; and the sweetness of one's friend is [the fruit] of hearty counsel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine own friend, and thy father's friend, forsake not; and go not into thy brother's house in the day of thy calamity: better is a neighbour that is near than a brother far off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be wise, my son, and make my heart glad, that I may have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A prudent [man] seeth the evil, [and] hideth himself; the simple pass on, [and] are punished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take his garment that is become surety [for] another, and hold him in pledge for a strange woman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that blesseth his friend with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, it shall be reckoned a curse to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A continual dropping on a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whosoever will restrain her restraineth the wind, and his right hand encountereth oil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Iron is sharpened by iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso keepeth the fig-tree shall eat the fruit thereof; and he that guardeth his master shall be honoured.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As [in] water face [answereth] to face, so the heart of man to man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sheol and destruction are insatiable; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fining-pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold; so let a man be to the mouth that praiseth him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou shouldest bray a fool in a mortar among wheat with a pestle, yet will not his folly depart from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be well acquainted with the appearance of thy flocks; look well to thy herds:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for wealth is not for ever; and doth the crown [endure] from generation to generation?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The hay is removed, and the tender grass sheweth itself, and herbs of the mountains are gathered in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lambs are for thy clothing, and the goats are the price of a field;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there is goats' milk enough for thy food, for the food of thy household, and sustenance for thy maidens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wicked flee when no man pursueth; but the righteous are bold as a lion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By the transgression of a land many are the princes thereof; but by a man of understanding [and] of knowledge, [its] stability is prolonged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A poor man who oppresseth the helpless is a sweeping rain which leaveth no food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that forsake the law praise the wicked; but such as keep the law contend with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Evil men understand not judgment; but they that seek Jehovah understand everything.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Better is the poor that walketh in his integrity, than he that is perverse, double in ways, though he be rich.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso observeth the law is a son that hath understanding; but he that is a companion of profligates bringeth shame to his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that by usury and unjust gain increaseth his substance gathereth it for him that is gracious to the poor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that turneth away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso causeth the upright to go astray in an evil way, shall himself fall into his own pit; but the perfect shall inherit good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A rich man is wise in his own eyes; but the poor that hath understanding searcheth him out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory; but when the wicked rise, men conceal themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that covereth his transgressions shall not prosper; but whoso confesseth and forsaketh [them] shall obtain mercy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Happy is the man that feareth always; but he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A roaring lion, and a ranging bear, is a wicked ruler over a poor people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The prince void of intelligence is also a great oppressor: he that hateth covetousness shall prolong [his] days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man laden with the blood of [any] person, fleeth to the pit: let no man stay him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso walketh in integrity shall be saved; but he that is perverted in [his] double ways, shall fall in one [of them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread; but he that followeth the worthless shall have poverty enough.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A faithful man aboundeth with blessings; but he that maketh haste to be rich shall not be innocent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To have respect of persons is not good; but for a piece of bread will a man transgress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hath an evil eye hasteth after wealth, and knoweth not that poverty shall come upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that rebuketh a man shall afterwards find more favour than he that flattereth with the tongue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso robbeth his father and his mother, and saith, It is no transgression, the same is the companion of a destroyer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is puffed up in soul exciteth contention; but he that relieth upon Jehovah shall be made fat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that confideth in his own heart is a fool; but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack; but he that withdraweth his eyes shall have many a curse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves; but when they perish, the righteous increase.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and without remedy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the righteous increase, the people rejoice; but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father; but he that is a companion of harlots destroyeth [his] substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A king by just judgment establisheth the land; but he that taketh gifts overthroweth it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his steps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare; but the righteous shall sing and rejoice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous taketh knowledge of the cause of the poor; the wicked understandeth not knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Scornful men set the city in a flame; but the wise turn away anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a wise man contendeth with a fool, whether he rage or laugh, [he] hath no rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The bloodthirsty hate the perfect, but the upright care for his soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A fool uttereth all his mind; but a wise [man] keepeth it back.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a ruler hearken to lying words, all his servants are wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The indigent and the oppressor meet together; Jehovah lighteneth the eyes of them both.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A king that faithfully judgeth the poor, his throne shall be established for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The rod and reproof give wisdom; but a child left [to himself] bringeth his mother to shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the wicked increase, transgression increaseth; but the righteous shall see their fall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Chasten thy son, and he shall give thee rest, and shall give delight unto thy soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where there is no vision the people cast off restraint; but happy is he that keepeth the law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A servant is not corrected by words: he understandeth indeed, but he will not answer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou seen a man hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child, shall in the end have him as a son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An angry man exciteth contention; and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A man's pride bringeth him low; but the humble in spirit shall obtain honour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoso shareth with a thief hateth his own soul: he heareth the adjuration, and declareth not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fear of man bringeth a snare; but whoso putteth his confidence in Jehovah is protected.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many seek the ruler's face; but a man's right judgment is from Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: An unjust man is an abomination to the righteous; and he that is of upright way is an abomination to the wicked [man].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh; the prophecy uttered by the man unto Ithiel, [even] unto Ithiel and Ucal:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Truly I am more stupid than any one; and I have not a man's intelligence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have neither learned wisdom, nor have I the knowledge of the Holy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath ascended up into the heavens, and descended? Who hath gathered the wind in his fists? Who hath bound the waters in a mantle? Who hath established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is his son's name, if thou knowest?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every word ofGod is pure: he is a shield unto them that put their trust in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee, and thou be found a liar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Two things do I ask of thee; deny me [them] not before I die:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remove far from me vanity and lies; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the bread of my daily need:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest I be full and deny [thee], and say, Who is Jehovah? or lest I be poor and steal, and outrage the name of myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak not too much about a servant to his master, lest he curse thee, and thou be held guilty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is a generation that curseth their father, and doth not bless their mother;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: there is a generation that are pure in their own eyes, yet are not washed from their filthiness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: there is a generation, — how lofty are their eyes, how their eyelids are lifted up!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — a generation whose teeth are swords, and their jaw-teeth knives, to devour the afflicted from off the earth, and the needy from [among] men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The leech hath two daughters: Give, give. There are three [things] never satisfied; four which say not, It is enough:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Sheol, and the barren womb; the earth which is not filled with water, and the fire which saith not, It is enough.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eye that mocketh at a father, and despiseth to obey a mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young eagles shall eat it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There are three [things] too wonderful for me, and four that I know not:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The way of an eagle in the heavens, the way of a serpent upon a rock, the way of a ship in the midst of the sea, and the way of a man with a maid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Such is the way of an adulterous woman: she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I have done no wickedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Under three [things] the earth is disquieted, and under four it cannot bear up:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Under a servant when he reigneth, and a churl when he is filled with meat;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: under an odious [woman] when she is married, and a handmaid when she is heir to her mistress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There are four [things] little upon the earth, and they are exceeding wise:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The ants, a people not strong, yet they provide their food in the summer;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the rock-badgers are but a feeble folk, yet they make their house in the cliff;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the locusts have no king, yet they go forth all of them by bands;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou takest hold of the lizard with the hands, yet is she in kings' palaces.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There are three [things] which have a stately step, and four are comely in going:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lion, mighty among beasts, which turneth not away for any;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a [horse] girt in the loins; or the he-goat; and a king, against whom none can rise up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou hast done foolishly in lifting up thyself, or if thou hast thought evil, [lay] the hand upon thy mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the pressing of milk bringeth forth butter, and the pressing of the nose bringeth forth blood; and the pressing of anger bringeth forth strife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The words of king Lemuel, the prophecy that his mother taught him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What, my son? and what, O son of my womb? and what, O son of my vows?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give not thy strength unto women, nor thy ways to them that destroy kings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is not for kings, Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, nor for rulers [to say], Where is the strong drink?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — lest they drink and forget the law, and pervert the judgment of any of the children of affliction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give strong drink unto him that is ready to perish, and wine unto the bitter of soul:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Open thy mouth for the dumb, for the cause of all those that are left desolate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and minister justice to the afflicted and needy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who can find a woman of worth? for her price is far above rubies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heart of her husband confideth in her, and he shall have no lack of spoil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She doeth him good, and not evil, all the days of her life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She seeketh wool and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She is like the merchants' ships: she bringeth her food from afar;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she riseth while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and the day's work to her maidens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She considereth a field, and acquireth it; of the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She girdeth her loins with strength, and maketh strong her arms.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She perceiveth that her earning is good; her lamp goeth not out by night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She putteth her hands to the distaff, and her hands hold the spindle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She stretcheth out her hand to the afflicted, and she reacheth forth her hands to the needy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She is not afraid of the snow for her household; for all her household are clothed with scarlet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She maketh herself coverlets; her clothing is byssus and purple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She maketh body linen and selleth it, and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Strength and dignity are her clothing, and she laugheth [at] the coming day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and upon her tongue is the law of kindness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She surveyeth the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her children rise up and call her blessed; her husband [also], and he praiseth her:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many daughters have done worthily, but thou excellest them all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gracefulness is deceitful and beauty is vain; a woman [that] feareth Jehovah, she shall be praised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give her of the fruit of her hands, and let her own works praise her in the gates.
